description_long: This object is a thin, rectangular electronic device resembling a digital book or e-reader, with a matte black exterior. The front cover displays the title 'THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF COMMON DISEASES' and the publisher 'COLTAN PRESS' in gold or light-colored text. The edges are sharp and the surface appears smooth, with no visible buttons, screens, or ports on the exterior. The spine and back are also black, with minimal markings, suggesting a sleek, minimalist design. The object is compact and flat, similar in size and shape to a large hardcover book. Its construction suggests it is intended for handheld or desktop use, likely for reading or reference purposes.
description: A sleek, black, book-shaped electronic device with gold text on the cover, resembling a digital encyclopedia or e-reader.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thin, flat profile and the gold text along the spine.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the full title and publisher in gold text on the black cover.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the slimness and the continuation of the gold text.
description_view_3: Back view with minimal markings, showing the plain black surface.
